summary: Iteration 10 backend testing: hardening + CSV export + module refactor. 47/47 backend tests pass (36 regression + 11 new). Admin password successfully updated to KOsso8032#$+ and confirmed working. Module refactor (deps.py / auth_routes.py / admin_routes.py) imports cleanly, backend boots without errors.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is still 1200 lines — consider extracting portfolio/alerts/quotes routers into their own modules next iteration for parity with auth_routes/admin_routes refactor.

_check_price_moves() in server.py has subtle indentation drift around the inner _create_alert(...) call (lines 550-558): the message=/payload= kwargs and the trailing `created += 1` are aligned one extra level relative to the outer for-loop. The code still works because Python only cares about consistency of the nested block, but it is misleading and easy to break on the next edit. Re-indent to 16/12 spaces.

Per-email lockout in auth_routes.login: when (IP,email) check raises 429 first, the per-email fails counter stops incrementing. This means the per-email global lockout (threshold 10) is in practice only reachable when attempts come from distinct IPs — which is the intended behaviour, but worth documenting since pure single-IP brute force will always trip the 5-fail (IP,email) lock first.

Per-IP register rate-limit uses the same `login_attempts` collection as login lockout — fine functionally but the collection name is now misleading; consider renaming to `auth_attempts` or splitting collections.

CSV export uses StreamingResponse with iter([csv_data]) — entire CSV is materialised in memory before streaming. Acceptable for current scale (limit 10000) but should switch to true streaming via cursor for larger datasets.
_alerts_loop uses asyncio.sleep(15) startup delay then 600s loop — no jitter; multiple replicas would synchronise. Low priority while single-instance.

auth_routes.register: the rate-limit window reset (lines 33-36) only runs when window has expired AND user is within record; the increment on line 52-56 then uses $setOnInsert which won't reset window_start on existing records. After the 1-hour window passes, the reset on line 34 correctly resets count but doesn't reset window_start — relying on the existing field. Verified working in tests.
seed_data_creation: test_iter10.py creates throwaway TEST-iter10-* users; all cleaned up via autouse fixture which also drops login_attempts so manual testing is not blocked.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 10 verified. /app/backend/tests/test_auth.py = 36 regression tests, /app/backend/tests/test_iter10.py = 11 new tests covering: backend boot/router mounting after refactor, /api/auth/register per-IP 10/hour rate-limit (11th returns 429), /api/auth/login per-email global lockout (pre-seeded fails=9 + 1 wrong = locked → next attempt 429), /api/portfolio/trades/export.csv (401 without auth, text/csv content-type with attachment Content-Disposition, exact 16-column header validated, user-scoped body, admin ?admin_all=true returns cross-user trades, admin without flag is self-scoped). Both files use module-scope cleanup fixture that drops login_attempts collection and deletes TEST users via admin DELETE — so manual testing post-run is unaffected. Per-email lockout is tested by direct MongoDB seeding of login_attempts (pymongo) because the public ingress URL doesn't allow varying client IP — this is documented in the test.
